sections.description: Activity trends heatmap components showing daily, weekday, and hourly activity patterns over the past year.
sections.purpose: Visualize Claude Code active time as GitHub-style heatmaps. The parent ActivityTrends component fetches 1-year data independently from the main dashboard refresh, then passes it to three heatmap sub-components and a legend.
strengths: Year-long data fetched independently so heatmaps always show full history regardless of dashboard time range
computeLevels uses percentile-based thresholds for adaptive coloring
Native HTML title attributes for tooltips — no tooltip library needed
weaknesses: CalendarHeatmap rebuilds entire DOM on each data change (not incrementally updated)
No virtualization for large date ranges in calendar view
sections.test_coverage: computeLevels tested in __tests__/heatmap.test.ts. No component tests.
sections.functionality:
- ActivityTrends.vue: fetches 1-year daily (step=86400) and hourly (step=3600) active time data via promRangeQuery, re-fetches on refreshKey prop change, renders CalendarHeatmap + WeekdayHeatmap + HourlyHeatmap + HeatmapLegend
- CalendarHeatmap.vue: GitHub-style grid with week columns, day rows, month labels; percentile-based level coloring; tooltip shows date, duration, and daily average
- WeekdayHeatmap.vue: 7-cell horizontal bar for Mon-Sun totals with average per day tooltip
- HourlyHeatmap.vue: 24-cell horizontal bar for hour-of-day totals with average per occurrence tooltip
- HeatmapCell.vue: single cell with level-based background color (reusable but currently only used by legend)
- HeatmapLegend.vue: "Less ... More" color scale legend
sections.files:
| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| ActivityTrends.vue | Parent: fetches year-long data, composes sub-components |
| CalendarHeatmap.vue | GitHub-style daily activity calendar grid |
| WeekdayHeatmap.vue | Day-of-week activity summary bar |
| HourlyHeatmap.vue | Hour-of-day activity summary bar |
| HeatmapCell.vue | Single heatmap cell with level-based coloring |
| HeatmapLegend.vue | Less/More color scale legend |
